intro.lead: One ecosystem, three experiences for clients, consumers, and operators: intelligence, experience, and operating performance — designed to win pitches, RFPs, and CoDesign workshops.

context.headline: The backdrop corporate services operate in
context.sub: Numbers leaders recognise: slower growth, persistent inflation, workforce pressure, and rising expectations on wellbeing and experience.

stat1.label: Global GDP growth outlook
stat1.hint: A modest baseline; efficiency and differentiation matter more than volume.

stat2.label: Inflation pressure
stat2.hint: Cost discipline and productivity are non-negotiable in contracts.

stat3.label: Global job gap by 2030
stat3.hint: Labour scarcity makes forecasting, staffing precision, and retention critical.

stat4.label: Employees expect more wellbeing investment
stat4.hint: Experience quality is now part of value, not a perk.

p1.title: Pioneering elevated experiences
p1.body: Human, digital, and physical experiences that employees and guests actually feel — measured, repeatable, and aligned to client outcomes.

p2.title: Continuous performance with embedded AI
p2.body: Decision-grade data, forecasting, and automation woven into operations — so teams spend time on judgment, not spreadsheets.

p3.title: Proving the value of partnership
p3.body: Transparent ROI, service benchmarks, and innovation you can take to the board — not slide-deck promises.

layers.headline: Three audience layers, one portfolio
layers.sub: Spark IQ, Spark XP, and Spark OS map how we win intelligence, experience, and operator outcomes — and how stories should be told in the room.

iq.title: Spark IQ
iq.body: Client intelligence and real-time insight — forecasting, sensing demand, and turning data into decisions across sites.

xp.title: Spark XP
xp.body: Elevated everyday experiences — front-stage journeys that improve satisfaction, adoption, and brand perception.

os.title: Spark OS
os.body: The operator’s platform — workflow, compliance, efficiency, and ROI you can audit and scale.

businessGoals.headline: What clients are solving for
businessGoals.sub: From the Standard Offer: the right experiences to support each client’s strategy — mapped across Spark IQ, Spark XP, and Spark OS.
businessGoals.bridge: Below, standard solutions in the 70% bucket are linked to real personas and journey moments in the Experience Catalogue.

g1.title: Drive retention & productivity
g1.body: Elevated everyday experiences that employees and guests feel — wellbeing, engagement, and talent outcomes clients can measure.

g2.title: Service efficiency & ROI
g2.body: Operational discipline, transparent performance, and cost control — with ROI and productivity gains you can defend in procurement.

g3.title: CSR & sustainability
g3.body: Environmental and social performance made visible at site level — scoring, reporting, and continuous improvement clients can audit.

g4.title: Extend asset life & reduce TCO
g4.body: Device, space, and workflow data brought together for reliable operations and lower total cost of ownership across the estate.

offerings.headline: What we put in front of clients
offerings.highlightLabel: Named solutions across Food, Hospitality, and Workplace / FM — filterable by outcome.

o1.title: Outcome-led solution lines
o1.body: Each storyline is built around business impact: retention and productivity, efficiency and ROI, CSR and sustainability, asset life — with KPIs you can defend in procurement.

o2.title: CoDesign in 90 days
o2.body: A structured Design Thinking path from problem framing to prototype, so workshops produce decisions — not sticky notes.

o3.title: Collective intelligence at scale
o3.body: Global factories and domain experts connected through common tooling — so delivery matches the narrative in every region.

o4.title: Data-centric by default
o4.body: Azure-aligned platforms and API-ready services — built to sit inside enterprise architecture, not beside it.

proof.headline: What clients say in the room

q1.quote: A partner who brings data, design, and delivery together — not a vendor deck, a joint plan.

q2.quote: We finally had operations, HR, and procurement looking at the same numbers — and the same roadmap.

q3.quote: The CoDesign sprint turned a tender requirement into a pilot with owners — in a single quarter.

ecosystem.headline: The engine behind the story
ecosystem.body: Thousands of experts, shared platforms, and repeatable playbooks — so the experience in the pitch is the experience in delivery.

m1.label: Experts in data, digital, AI, and transformation

m2.label: Digital and AI factories across major regions

ecosystem.closing: Use this deck to align the room: one narrative, three layers, outcomes that survive diligence — making millions of small moments matter.

roadmap.headline: From concepts to a matched roadmap
roadmap.sub: A balanced approach for Innovation, Digital, and AI: immediate impact, tailored delivery, and selective pilots — aligned to short, mid, and long-term horizons.
roadmap.tiersNote: The same logic applies across the XP Catalogue: ~25 standard solutions in the 70% bucket, adapted variants in 20%, and tested innovations in 10% — all discoverable by persona and journey moment.

pathChoice.title: How do you want to explore the portfolio?
pathChoice.lead: For sales: pick the entry point that matches how the client thinks — same portfolio, same 70 / 20 / 10 split behind both paths.

business.title: Start from business objectives
business.body: Open with client outcomes and Spark layers — then the 70% standard solutions, roadmap logic, proof, and CoDesign in this deck.

experience.title: Start from experience (XP)
experience.body: Open with personas and journey moments — then browse 90+ solutions in the XP Catalogue (standard, adapted, and innovation on the same 70 / 20 / 10 basis).

pathChoice.footnote: Both paths cover the same portfolio. The 25 standard solutions are the 70% core; pilot innovations (10%) sit in the catalogue with client-scoped access.

roadmap.outcome: Output: prioritised roadmap, business case per initiative, and implementation plan (quick wins / mid-term / long-term).

70.title: Standard offer
70.body: Sodexo existing services, deployable now. Proven, costed, and the backbone of most client roadmaps.
70.focus: Pragmatic, high-impact initiatives with digital and AI already in market.

20.title: Adapted standard
20.body: Light customisation of existing services — site configuration, service model, and digital layer tuned to the client.
20.focus: Enhance core capabilities and engagement without reinventing the stack.

10.title: Innovation / pilot
10.body: One or two new solutions tested on a pilot site — lower certainty, higher upside, with a defined test protocol.
10.focus: Available with selected clients; explore via CoDesign and catalogue (gated).

journeyStrip.headline: Standard solutions on real personas
journeyStrip.sub: Grouped by the client outcomes above — {count} solutions in the 70% bucket, each mapped to a persona and journey moment in the XP Catalogue where available.
journeyStrip.catalogueOnly: Catalogue detail — journey mapping coming soon

legend.title: Where persona, experience & journey belong
legend.sub: A wireframe recreation of the Spark OS SharePoint page. Three dashed zones show our recommended insert points — toggle each preview to see what we'd add.

page.heroLead: Digital operations, AI automation, and site performance — the platform layer operators rely on every day.
page.stat1: Global FM & workplace services market — efficiency at scale matters.
page.stat2: Of operators cite tool fragmentation as a top frustration.
page.stat3: Potential productivity gain when workflows are unified on one platform.
page.introHeadline: New pressures. New priorities. New ways to operate.
page.introBody: Clients expect measurable ROI, compliant operations, and elevated experiences — often with fewer people on the ground. Spark OS answers with intelligence, automation, and operator-first design.
page.prioritiesLabel: What operations teams are prioritizing today:
page.transitionBody: Capability areas map to modular solutions — but only make sense when tied to who uses them and when.
page.ctaTitle: Looking for another challenge to solve?
page.ctaBody: Explore Spark IQ for client intelligence or Spark OS for operator workflows — each layer connects to the same journey.

persona.rationale: Insert here — right after the macro stats, before the narrative deepens. Humanises Spark OS immediately: who is the operator, what do they need, why should the reader care?
persona.title: Site Manager · Food & FM
persona.description: Runs multiple client sites — orchestrating teams, schedules, compliance, and client relationships. On-site most of the day; needs one platform, not three tools that don't talk.
persona.quote: When there's a problem, I need quick info and quick solutions. I don't have time for tools that don't sync.

experience.rationale: Insert inside the intro section — bridge from market pressures to client outcomes. Keywords anchor each business goal (G1–G4) so solutions aren't presented in a vacuum.
experience.headline: The right experiences for each business goal
experience.body: Before listing capabilities, name what clients are solving for — then map Spark OS modules to those outcomes.

goals.g1: Elevated everyday experiences → wellbeing, engagement, retention KPIs.
goals.g2: Operational discipline → ROI, productivity, cost control clients can defend.
goals.g3: Site-level CSR → scoring, reporting, continuous improvement.
goals.g4: Asset & workflow data → reliable ops, lower TCO across the estate.

journey.rationale: Insert between the capability grid and Our Solutions — the pivot from what we offer to how it fits a working day. Links modules to moments: commute, kick-off, rounds, planning, service.
journey.headline: Explore each moment of the working day:
journey.body: The puzzle path connects Operator moments to Spark OS modules — choose what to implement and craft seamless experiences.

legend.title: Where persona, experience & journey belong on WRX
legend.sub: A wireframe recreation of the Sodexo WRX product page (Spark XP). Three dashed zones show recommended insert points — toggle each preview to see what we'd add.

page.heroLead: A digital workspace experience built to simplify daily life and drive engagement.
page.whyBody1: Employees expect the same seamless digital experience at work that they get as consumers. WRX brings food, space, access, and wellbeing services into one mobile-first app.
page.whyBody2: For clients, it means higher adoption of on-site services, measurable satisfaction, and data that feeds operational decisions — not another siloed tool.
page.resourcesSub: Everything sales and delivery teams need to pitch, demo, and implement WRX.

faq.q1: How does Sodexo WRX differ from a simple app?
faq.a1: WRX is a modular experience platform — not a single feature app. It connects food ordering, space booking, building access, and wellbeing into one journey, with modules clients can adopt progressively.
faq.q2: Who is Sodexo WRX meant for?
faq.a2: Primary users are employees and guests on corporate campuses (white-collar, hybrid, lab-based). WRX is sold to clients solving for retention, productivity, and elevated workplace experience.
faq.q3: How does WRX connect to the Experience Catalogue?
faq.a3: Each WRX module maps to a persona moment in the XP Catalogue — commute, welcome, workplace, F&B, wellbeing — so pitches stay grounded in real journeys.

page.ctaTitle: Looking for another challenge to solve?
page.ctaBody: Explore related Spark XP solutions or cross-sell into Spark IQ and Spark OS layers.

persona.rationale: Insert here — right after "Why Sodexo WRX?", before value pillars. Grounds the product in a real user: who eats, books, and moves through the campus every day.
persona.title: Admin Function · White Collar
persona.description: Works at an international R&D centre — lab-based and nomadic (~50%), desk and ABW (~40%), some remote. Needs quick, healthy food and seamless services or he'll go elsewhere.
persona.quote: I need quick, healthy, and affordable food at work, with little extras that make my day easier — otherwise I'll just go elsewhere.

experience.rationale: Insert inside "What WRX makes possible" — alongside Features and Business Impact. Name the experience outcomes and keywords before listing capabilities; ties WRX to client business goals.
experience.headline: The experience factor — why WRX wins
experience.body: WRX isn't just features — it's the front-stage experience that drives retention, adoption, and measurable client outcomes.

goals.g1: Retention & productivity → healthy meals, focus, work-life balance on busy days.
goals.g1b: Adoption & satisfaction → digital tools people actually use; NPS and daily active users.
goals.g3: CSR & sustainability → sustainable meal options, values alignment employees expect.
goals.g1c: Seamless digital experience → one app for food, space, access — no buggy silos.

journey.rationale: Insert between "What makes possible" and Resources — bridges product capabilities to daily moments. Shows which WRX modules activate at commute, welcome, workplace, F&B, wellbeing.
journey.headline: Explore each moment of the working day:
journey.body: The puzzle connects Consumer moments to WRX modules — digital ordering, vending, concierge, delivery, wellbeing — pick what to implement.
